title: AAOS × MiniMind issue #9 requirement-to-artifact completion evidence

implementation_status_definitions.implemented: The scoped requirement has a maintained artifact and evidence reference.
implementation_status_definitions.implemented_as_skeleton: Only a descriptive or structural skeleton exists; no full runtime implementation is claimed.
implementation_status_definitions.renamed: The material artifact exists at a different maintained path with traceable evidence.
implementation_status_definitions.superseded: A later maintained artifact explicitly replaces the declared artifact.
implementation_status_definitions.placeholder: A path exists but contains non-material or generic placeholder content.
implementation_status_definitions.missing: No maintained artifact or authorized deferral is recorded.
implementation_status_definitions.deferred: The requirement is intentionally unimplemented and includes an explicit reason.
implementation_status_definitions.not_applicable: The requirement does not apply to the maintained adapter scope and includes an explanation.

requirements.requirement: Position MiniMind as a local model runtime specimen, controlled inference target, teaching substrate, verification subject, and model-swappability baseline.
requirements.notes: The positioning exists only as descriptive M2 adapter skeleton language; no MiniMind runtime or inference implementation is present.

requirements.requirement: Route MiniMind only through the declared L3, L4, L4.5, and L6 candidate or subject roles.
requirements.notes: Layer routing is documented in the M2 adapter skeleton and radar specimen, but no runtime route is implemented.

requirements.requirement: Preserve the L0/L1/L2 sovereignty boundary and prohibit MiniMind from acquiring governance, policy, identity, decision, verification, audit, safety, or release authority.
requirements.notes: Authority invariants are documented in the M2 adapter skeleton and statically checked by the repository-truth regression; they do not constitute a runtime enforcement implementation.
declared_items: Model runtime is not governance authority.
Local inference is not sovereignty proof.
White-box access is not audit closure.
Training pipeline visibility is not safety proof.
Model response is not decision approval.
Tool-use behavior is not workflow governance.
Agentic RL sample is not production agent authority.
Small-model controllability is not policy compliance.
Verification target is not verification authority.
MiniMind is an L3 runtime specimen, not an AAOS L0/L1/L2 authority.

requirements.requirement: Define the ten named MiniMind evidence-record families.
requirements.notes: All ten names are catalogued in the M2 adapter skeleton, but record structures, schemas, and instances are not implemented.

requirements.requirement: Bind MiniMind admission evidence to all fields declared in issue #9.
requirements.notes: The M2 adapter skeleton mirrors only 16 of the 25 declared fields. It additionally names a human reviewer but does not implement required_human_review semantics; no record schema enforces any field.

requirements.requirement: Define the exact MiniMind evidence-category taxonomy declared in issue #9.
requirements.deferred_reason: The maintained M2 skeleton names record files but does not define the exact evidence-category taxonomy; #235 does not expand the adapter implementation.
requirements.notes: This taxonomy remains explicitly deferred and is not inferred from similarly named record files.

requirements.requirement: Define the exact MiniMind risk-category taxonomy declared in issue #9.
requirements.deferred_reason: The maintained M2 skeleton documents risks narratively but does not define the exact machine-readable risk categories; #235 is limited to completion evidence.
requirements.notes: Narrative fail-closed risks are not treated as an implemented risk taxonomy.

requirements.requirement: Support the five declared AAOS-compatible MiniMind evaluator outcomes.
requirements.notes: The five outcomes are vocabulary in the M2 adapter skeleton only; no MiniMind admission or runtime evaluator emits them.

requirements.requirement: Apply the ten declared fail-closed conditions to MiniMind evidence and authority claims.
requirements.notes: The fail-closed conditions are documented by the M2 adapter skeleton but are not implemented as MiniMind runtime evaluators.
declared_items: model output treated as governance decision
local inference treated as safety proof
tool-use experiment treated as production tool permission
training visibility treated as audit closure
runtime trace lacks identity, config, prompt, timestamp, or hash binding
MiniMind defines L0/L1/L2 policy or approval boundaries
model swappability not demonstrated under one governance contract

requirements.requirement: Keep MiniMind as the object of verification-harness tests and never the owner of criteria, thresholds, fail-closed policy, risk calibration, or audit closure.
requirements.notes: The verification-subject role and candidate test uses are documented in the M2 adapter skeleton; no verification harness is implemented.

requirements.requirement: Demonstrate model swappability under one stable governance contract.
requirements.notes: The MiniMind-to-larger-model comparison is documented as an M2 adapter skeleton concept only; no model is loaded and no swappability evaluation runs.
declared_items: behavior comparison under identical decision boundary

requirements.requirement: Preserve issue #9 non-goals, including no MiniMind implementation, training, output approval, production deployment approval, release approval, or audit closure.
requirements.notes: The non-goals are explicitly documented in the M2 adapter skeleton and preserved by this static repair.
declared_items: do not treat MiniMind as verification authority
do not treat local inference as sovereignty proof
do not define AAOS L0/L1/L2 policy
do not approve production agent deployment

requirements.requirement: Test admit_with_constraints, review_required, allow_with_recorded_exception, reject, and fail_closed MiniMind evaluator cases.
requirements.deferred_reason: No MiniMind functional evaluator exists to exercise these outcomes. #235 adds repository-truth coverage only and must not claim runtime test completion.
requirements.notes: The new regression tests completion evidence and placeholder substitution, not MiniMind behavior.
